codeReview.summary: The feature adds a solid 5-state Playwright visual regression suite and DS primitive checks, but the core parity assertion is against app-generated baselines rather than igloo-paper source screenshots required by the contract.

issues.description: Parity checks use `toHaveScreenshot('dashboard-<state>.png')` against locally committed snapshots (generated from the app/demo itself), not against igloo-paper screenshots. This can lock in existing drift and does not satisfy the feature/VAL-CROSS-017 requirement to diff dashboard states against igloo-paper source frames.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ui-screen-worker skill says omitting agent-browser must set `followedProcedure: false`, but this handoff records `followedProcedure: true` while also listing an explicit agent-browser omission. The skill/procedure contract is ambiguous for test-only features and should be clarified/enforced.

Mission guidance says 'No `.skip` additions', but established e2e practice uses project-gated `test.skip(testInfo.project.name !== "desktop")`; this feature followed that same pattern. The rule appears too broad and should clarify whether conditional project gating is acceptable.

summary: Fail: while the new visual regression suite is comprehensive for five dashboard states, it validates against self-generated snapshots rather than direct igloo-paper frames, so strict paper-parity validation is not fully satisfied.
